name: edgedb
documentation:
# The Python driver for EdgeDB

```{image} https://github.com/edgedb/edgedb-python/workflows/Tests/badge.svg?event=push&branch=master
:target: https://github.com/edgedb/edgedb-python/actions
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/edgedb.svg
:target: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/edgedb
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/badge/join-github%20discussions-green
:target: https://github.com/edgedb/edgedb/discussions
```

**edgedb-python** is the official EdgeDB driver for Python.
It provides both blocking IO and asyncio implementations.

The library requires Python 3.8 or later.

## Documentation

The project documentation can be found
[here](https://edgedb.com/docs/clients/00_python/index).

## Installation

The library is available on PyPI. Use `pip` to install it:

```
$ pip install edgedb
```

## Basic Usage

```python
import datetime
import edgedb

def main():
    client = edgedb.create_client()
    # Create a User object type
    client.execute('''
        CREATE TYPE User {
            CREATE REQUIRED PROPERTY name -> str;
            CREATE PROPERTY dob -> cal::local_date;
        }
    ''')

    # Insert a new User object
    client.query('''
        INSERT User {
            name := <str>$name,
            dob := <cal::local_date>$dob
        }
    ''', name='Bob', dob=datetime.date(1984, 3, 1))

    # Select User objects.
    user_set = client.query(
        'SELECT User {name, dob} FILTER .name = <str>$name', name='Bob')
    # *user_set* now contains
    # Set{Object{name := 'Bob', dob := datetime.date(1984, 3, 1)}}

    # Close the client.
    client.close()

if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()
```

## Development

Instructions for installing EdgeDB and edgedb-python locally can be found at
[edgedb.com/docs/reference/dev](https://edgedb.com/docs/reference/dev).

To run the test suite, run `$ python setup.py test`.

## License

edgedb-python is developed and distributed under the Apache 2.0 license.

functions.name: edgedb.scram.build_verifier
documentation:
Build the SCRAM verifier for the given password.

Returns a string in the following format:

    "<MECHANISM>$<iterations>:<salt>$<StoredKey>:<ServerKey>"

The salt and keys are base64-encoded values.

functions.name: edgedb.scram.verify_password
documentation:
Check the given password against a verifier.

Returns True if the password is OK, False otherwise.

functions.name: edgedb.scram.B64
documentation: Return base64-encoded string representation of input binary data.

limitations under the License.
This file MUST NOT contain anything but the __version__ assignment.
When making a release, change the value of __version__
to an appropriate value, and open a pull request against
the correct branch (master if making a new feature release).
The commit message MUST contain a properly formatted release
log, and the commit must be signed.
The release automation will: build and test the packages for the
supported platforms, publish the packages on PyPI, merge the PR
to the target branch, create a Git tag pointing to the commit.

methods.name: edgedb.blocking_client.BlockingIOConnection.close
documentation: Send graceful termination message wait for connection to drop.

classes.name: edgedb.blocking_client.Client

methods.name: edgedb.blocking_client.Client.close
documentation:
Attempt to gracefully close all connections in the client.

Wait until all pool connections are released, close them and
shut down the pool. If any error (including cancellation) occurs
in `close()` the pool will terminate by calling
Client.terminate() .

A lazy connection pool.

A Client can be used to manage a set of connections to the database.
Connections are first acquired from the pool, then used, and then released
back to the pool. Once a connection is released, it's reset to close all
open cursors and other resources *except* prepared statements.

Clients are created by calling
{func}`~edgedb.blocking_client.create_client`.

methods.name: edgedb.asyncio_client.AsyncIOConnection.aclose
documentation: Send graceful termination message wait for connection to drop.

methods.name: edgedb.asyncio_client._AsyncIOPoolImpl.aclose
documentation:
Attempt to gracefully close all connections in the pool.

Wait until all pool connections are released, close them and
shut down the pool. If any error (including cancellation) occurs
in `close()` the pool will terminate by calling
\_AsyncIOPoolImpl.terminate() .

It is advisable to use {func}`python:asyncio.wait_for` to set
a timeout.

classes.name: edgedb.asyncio_client.AsyncIOClient

methods.name: edgedb.asyncio_client.AsyncIOClient.aclose
documentation:
Attempt to gracefully close all connections in the pool.

Wait until all pool connections are released, close them and
shut down the pool. If any error (including cancellation) occurs
in `aclose()` the pool will terminate by calling
AsyncIOClient.terminate() .

It is advisable to use {func}`python:asyncio.wait_for` to set
a timeout.

A lazy connection pool.

A Client can be used to manage a set of connections to the database.
Connections are first acquired from the pool, then used, and then released
back to the pool. Once a connection is released, it's reset to close all
open cursors and other resources *except* prepared statements.

Clients are created by calling
{func}`~edgedb.asyncio_client.create_async_client`.

documentation:
Add a listener for EdgeDB log messages.

:param callable callback:
    A callable receiving the following arguments:
    **connection**: a Connection the callback is registered with;
    **message**: the `edgedb.EdgeDBMessage` message.

methods.name: edgedb.base_client.BaseConnection.remove_log_listener
documentation: Remove a listening callback for log messages.

methods.name: edgedb.base_client.BaseConnection.is_in_transaction
documentation:
Return True if Connection is currently inside a transaction.

:return bool: True if inside transaction, False otherwise.

methods.name: edgedb.base_client.PoolConnectionHolder._release
documentation: Release this connection holder.

methods.name: edgedb.base_client.BasePoolImpl.set_connect_args
documentation:
Set the new connection arguments for this pool.

The new connection arguments will be used for all subsequent
new connection attempts. Existing connections will remain until
they expire. Use BasePoolImpl.expire_connections() to expedite
the connection expiry.

```
param str dsn

Connection arguments specified using as a single string in
the following format:
edgedb://user:pass@host:port/database?option=value.

param **connect_kwargs

Keyword arguments for the
 function.
```

methods.name: edgedb.base_client.BasePoolImpl.terminate
documentation: Terminate all connections in the pool.

methods.name: edgedb.base_client.BasePoolImpl.expire_connections
documentation:
Expire all currently open connections.

Cause all currently open connections to get replaced on the
next query.

methods.name: edgedb.base_client.BaseClient.max_concurrency
documentation: Max number of connections in the pool.

methods.name: edgedb.base_client.BaseClient.free_size
documentation: Number of available connections in the pool.

methods.name: edgedb.base_client.BaseClient.terminate
documentation: Terminate all connections in the pool.

classes.name: edgedb.pgproto.types.BitString

methods.name: edgedb.pgproto.types.BitString.to_int
documentation:
Interpret the BitString as a Python int.
Acts similarly to int.from_bytes.

```
param bitorder

Determines the bit order used to interpret the BitString. By
default, this function uses Postgres conventions for casting bits
to ints. If bitorder is 'big', the most significant bit is at the
start of the string (this is the same as the default). If bitorder
is 'little', the most significant bit is at the end of the string.

param bool signed

Determines whether two's complement is used to interpret the
BitString. If signed is False, the returned value is always
non-negative.

return int

An integer representing the BitString. Information about
the BitString's exact length is lost.
```

:::{versionadded} 0.18.0
:::

methods.name: edgedb.pgproto.types.BitString.from_int
documentation:
Represent the Python int x as a BitString.
Acts similarly to int.to_bytes.

```
param int x

An integer to represent. Negative integers are represented in two's
complement form, unless the argument signed is False, in which case
negative integers raise an OverflowError.

param int length

The length of the resulting BitString. An OverflowError is raised
if the integer is not representable in this many bits.

param bitorder

Determines the bit order used in the BitString representation. By
default, this function uses Postgres conventions for casting ints
to bits. If bitorder is 'big', the most significant bit is at the
start of the string (this is the same as the default). If bitorder
is 'little', the most significant bit is at the end of the string.

param bool signed

Determines whether two's complement is used in the BitString
representation. If signed is False and a negative integer is given,
an OverflowError is raised.

return BitString

A BitString representing the input integer, in the
form specified by the other input args.
```

:::{versionadded} 0.18.0
:::
Immutable representation of PostgreSQL `bit` and `varbit` types.

classes.name: edgedb.options.TransactionOptions
documentation: Options for `transaction()`

methods.name: edgedb.options._OptionsMixin.with_transaction_options
documentation:
Returns object with adjusted options for future transactions.

```
param options TransactionOptions

Object that encapsulates transaction options.
```

This method returns a "shallow copy" of the current object
with modified transaction options.

Both `self` and returned object can be used after, but when using
them transaction options applied will be different.

Transaction options are used by the `transaction` method.

methods.name: edgedb.options._OptionsMixin.with_retry_options
documentation:
Returns object with adjusted options for future retrying
transactions.

```
param options RetryOptions

Object that encapsulates retry options.
```

This method returns a "shallow copy" of the current object
with modified retry options.

Both `self` and returned object can be used after, but when using
them retry options applied will be different.

methods.name: edgedb.options._OptionsMixin.with_warning_handler
documentation:
Returns object with adjusted options for handling warnings.

```
param warning_handler WarningHandler

Function for handling warnings. It is passed a tuple of warnings
and the query result and returns a potentially updated query
result.
```

This method returns a "shallow copy" of the current object
with modified retry options.

Both `self` and returned object can be used after, but when using
them retry options applied will be different.

classes.name: edgedb.options._Options
documentation: Internal class for storing connection options

classes.name: edgedb.errors.tags.Tag
documentation:
Error tag

Tags are used to differentiate certain properties of errors that apply to
error classes across hierarchy.

Use `error.has_tag(tag_name)` to check for a tag.

modules.name: edgedb.errors
documentation: AUTOGENERATED FROM "edb/api/errors.txt" WITH
$ edb gen-errors \
--import 'from edgedb.errors._base import *\nfrom edgedb.errors.tags import *' \
--extra-all "_base.__all__" \
--stdout \
--client
flake8: noqa

functions.name: edgedb.con_utils._extract_errno
documentation:
Extract multiple errnos from error string

When we connect to a host that has multiple underlying IP addresses, say
`localhost` having `::1` and `127.0.0.1`, we get
`OSError("Multiple exceptions:...")` error without `.errno` attribute
set. There are multiple ones in the text, so we extract all of them.

methods.name: edgedb.con_utils.ResolvedConnectConfig.database
documentation: The properties actually merge database and branch, but "default" is
different. If you need to know the underlying config use the _database
and _branch.

classes.name: tests.test_postgis.TestPostgis
documentation:
Test PostGIS extension.

The GEO data gets sent as WKB (or EWKB) binary format. Thus we wrap the
bytes into a simple object that has `wkb` field avaialable. Any library
that can work with GEOS data should be able to read this format.

Raw bytes used as sample GEOS data in (E)WKB format.
